task_completion.description: Construct a circle centered at the origin (0, 0) with radius 3
task_completion.visual_evidence: GeoGebra graphics or algebra view shows a circle centered at (0,0) passing through (3,0), (0,3), (-3,0), and (0,-3).

task_completion.description: Create fixed points A at (3, 0) and B at (0, 3)
task_completion.visual_evidence: Points labeled A and B are visible exactly at coordinates (3, 0) and (0, 3) respectively.

task_completion.description: Create a moveable point C constrained to the circle's major arc
task_completion.visual_evidence: A point labeled C is visible on the circle, distinct from A and B (typically in the 2nd, 3rd, or 4th quadrant).

task_completion.description: Measure and display the inscribed angle ∠ACB and the central angle ∠AOB
task_completion.visual_evidence: Angle indicators are visible in the graphics view or algebra view showing ∠AOB = 90° and ∠ACB = 45°. Segments connecting the points (A-O, B-O, A-C, B-C) should generally be visible to form the angles.

task_completion.description: Include a text annotation showing the relationship between the angles
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text box is visible in the GeoGebra graphics view stating the relationship, such as 'Central Angle = 2 * Inscribed Angle'.

task_completion.description: Save the completed construction as 'inscribed_angle.ggb' in ~/Documents/GeoGebra/projects/
task_completion.visual_evidence: A save dialog, terminal window, or file manager shows the file 'inscribed_angle.ggb' being saved or existing in the '~/Documents/GeoGebra/projects/' directory, or the GeoGebra window title reflects the filename.

integrity.description: The agent used the GeoGebra GUI to construct the geometry
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent clicking GeoGebra tools (Point, Circle, Angle, Text) and interacting with the canvas, rather than just running a script to generate the file.

integrity.description: Angles are genuinely measured by GeoGebra, not faked with static text
integrity.visual_evidence: The algebra view shows actual angle variables (e.g., α = 90°, β = 45°) and the graphics view displays standard GeoGebra angle arcs, rather than manually placed text boxes mimicking angle labels.

integrity.description: The file was saved authentically through the application or standard OS operations
integrity.visual_evidence: The GeoGebra interface shows a 'Save' dialog or the filename updates in the application title bar, proving it wasn't just copied from a pre-existing solution file.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The circle must be centered at (0,0) with radius 3.0. Point A is at (3.0, 0.0) and Point B is at (0.0, 3.0). The central angle ∠AOB must be exactly 90°. According to the Inscribed Angle Theorem, the inscribed angle ∠ACB must be exactly 45° (half of the central angle). The expected filename is 'inscribed_angle.ggb' located in ~/Documents/GeoGebra/projects/.
